task_completion.description: Navigate to the Emoncms Feeds page
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open showing the Emoncms interface, specifically navigated to the 'Feeds' section (usually accessible via the top menu or sidebar).
In the final screenshots of the Emoncms Feeds page, the feed named 'test_voltage_check' is no longer visible in the list.
In the final screenshots of the Emoncms Feeds page, the feed named 'test_ct_sensor_1' is no longer visible in the list.
In the final screenshots of the Emoncms Feeds page, the feed named 'test_calibration_run' is no longer visible in the list.
In the final screenshots of the Emoncms Feeds page, the feed named 'test_mqtt_connection' is no longer visible in the list.
In the final screenshots, other feeds that do not match the 4 specific test feed names are still present and visible in the Emoncms Feeds list.

integrity.description: Agent used the Emoncms web GUI to delete the feeds
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the Emoncms web interface (clicking checkboxes, clicking the delete button/trash icon) rather than executing curl commands, API requests, or database queries in a terminal.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Emoncms application
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser URL bar shows the local Emoncms instance address, and the interface matches standard Emoncms styling, not a text editor or mock page.

integrity.description: Agent selectively deleted feeds instead of wiping the database
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent selects specific rows in the UI to delete, and the final screenshot shows a functioning Emoncms interface with other feeds still intact, proving the database wasn't simply dropped or corrupted.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The exact 4 test feeds that must be deleted are: 'test_voltage_check', 'test_ct_sensor_1', 'test_calibration_run', and 'test_mqtt_connection'. All of these feeds are tagged with the word 'test'. Verify that these specific feeds are absent from the final feed list, while ensuring that other feeds (production feeds) remain untouched.
